title: adobe/react-spectrum – GitHub
insights: Monorepo containing React Aria, React Stately, React Spectrum, @internationalized packages. Key packages: @react-aria/interactions (usePress, useHover, useMove, useFocus, useKeyboard), @react-aria/focus (FocusScope, FocusRing, useFocusVisible), @react-aria/overlays (useOverlay, usePreventScroll, useOverlayPosition), @react-aria/selection (selection state management for listbox/table).

title: radix-ui/primitives – GitHub
insights: 18.6k stars. Source of truth for Radix implementation. Each primitive in packages/react/{name}. Key implementation files: Slot.tsx (asChild engine), DismissableLayer.tsx (click-outside + focus-out detection), FocusScope.tsx (focus trap + focus restoration), Portal.tsx (DOM portal mounting), Presence.tsx (animation-aware mount/unmount). These utilities are composable building blocks used across all Radix primitives.

title: w3c/aria-practices – GitHub
insights: Source repository for W3C APG. HTML + JS working examples for all 30+ patterns. Significant resource: examples/combobox/, examples/dialog-modal/, examples/listbox/, examples/menu-button/, examples/treeview/, examples/grid/, examples/tabs/. Each example is tested against NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver. Issue tracker reveals what AT combinations are problematic.

title: Accessibility – React Aria
insights: Adobe's accessibility philosophy document. React Aria handles keyboard/pointer/touch/screen reader in one unified interaction model. Mobile screen readers use virtual cursor gestures (swipe), not keyboard tab. useFocusVisible distinguishes keyboard focus from pointer focus to conditionally show focus rings per WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.11.

title: React Aria Hooks – Complete List
insights: Full hook catalog: useButton, useToggleButton, useCheckbox, useRadioGroup, useSwitch, useTextField, useSearchField, useNumberField, useSelect, useListBox, useComboBox, useMenu, useMenuTrigger, useDialog, useModal, useOverlay, useOverlayTrigger, useDatePicker, useCalendar, useRangeCalendar, useDateField, useTimeField, useTable, useGrid, useGridList, useTreeData, useSlider, useTabs, useTooltip, useTooltipTrigger, useSeparator, useMeter, useProgressBar, useToast, useBreadcrumbs.

title: React Aria – Adobe
insights: Adobe's hook-first headless primitive library. Provides 50+ hooks and components. Strictly adheres to WAI-ARIA APG. Separates state (react-stately) from DOM wiring (react-aria). Provides usePress, useHover, useMove, useFocus, useFocusVisible, useFocusRing, useKeyboard interaction hooks. Supports 30+ locales and 13 calendar systems via @internationalized/date.

title: Composition – Radix Primitives
insights: Official documentation for the asChild pattern. When asChild=true, Radix clones the single child element and injects all required ARIA attributes, event handlers, and ref — avoiding a superfluous wrapper DOM node. Replaces the polymorphic 'as' prop approach with a safer, type-sound alternative that does not require complex generic types.

title: Introduction – Radix Primitives
insights: Radix Primitives is the gold standard for unstyled React primitives. Each component is independently versioned, WAI-ARIA compliant, and ships the asChild prop via @radix-ui/react-slot so consumers can compose onto any element without DOM inflation. 28 core components including Dialog, DropdownMenu, Select, Tabs, Tooltip, Popover, Accordion, AlertDialog, Avatar, Checkbox, Collapsible, ContextMenu, HoverCard, Label, Menubar, NavigationMenu, RadioGroup, ScrollArea, Separator, Slider, Switch, Toast, Toggle, ToggleGroup, Toolbar. Maintained by WorkOS. 18.6k GitHub stars.

title: ARIA Authoring Practices Guide – W3C WAI
insights: The normative reference for ARIA implementation. 30+ design patterns with working JavaScript examples. Covers: Accordion, Alert, AlertDialog, Breadcrumb, Button, Carousel, Checkbox, Combobox, Dialog, Disclosure, Feed, Grid, GridCell, Landmark Regions, Link, Listbox, Menu/MenuButton, Meter, RadioGroup, Scrollbar, Separator, Slider, SpinButton, Switch, Table, Tabs, Toolbar, Tooltip, TreeView, TreeGrid, WindowSplitter.

title: Patterns – W3C APG (Full Pattern Index)
insights: Index of all 30+ APG patterns. Canonical entry point for discovering what ARIA patterns exist. Each links to: description, keyboard interaction, WAI-ARIA roles/states/properties, working examples. Updated continuously by W3C ARIA Working Group. Used as spec source by all major headless libraries.

title: Combobox Pattern – W3C APG
insights: ARIA 1.2 combobox: input with associated popup (listbox, grid, tree, or dialog). Popup type determines keyboard behavior. Arrow Down opens popup and moves to first option. Escape closes and returns focus to input. aria-expanded on input. aria-controls points to popup. aria-activedescendant tracks highlighted option in popup. Key difference from ARIA 1.1: aria-haspopup moved to input from wrapper.

title: Dialog (Modal) Pattern – W3C APG
insights: Normative dialog pattern. role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby. Focus must move into dialog on open. Focus trap cycles Tab/Shift+Tab within dialog. Initial focus: first interactive element or static header with tabindex=-1 if content risks scroll-out-of-view. Escape closes. Focus returns to trigger on close. inert attribute preferred over aria-hidden for background content.

title: Developing a Keyboard Interface – W3C APG
insights: The canonical guide for keyboard patterns. Two approaches: (1) roving tabindex — DOM focus moves, scrolls automatically, works in all AT; (2) aria-activedescendant — container holds focus, IDREF points to 'active' item, no auto-scroll, VoiceOver has known gaps. Tab/Shift+Tab enter/exit composite widgets. Arrow keys navigate within. Home/End, Page Up/Down optional.

title: Ariakit – Toolkit for Building Accessible UIs
insights: Direct successor to Reakit. Provider-based state. Supports controlled/uncontrolled. virtualFocus option makes the container hold real DOM focus while aria-activedescendant tracks logical active item — critical for combobox patterns where input must keep focus while list item appears active to AT.

title: API Reference – Ariakit
insights: Ariakit (successor of Reakit) provides lowest-level ARIA building blocks. Composite component is the keystone: manages roving tabindex or aria-activedescendant for any widget. CompositeProvider, CompositeItem, CompositeGroup, CompositeTypeahead. Adds Dialog, Disclosure, Menu, MenuButton, MenuItem, Popover, Select, Tab, TabList, TabPanel, Tooltip, TooltipAnchor.

title: Ark UI – Framework-Agnostic Headless Primitives
insights: 45+ components across React, Vue, Svelte, Solid. State machine foundation (Zag). WCAG tested with real AT. asChild pattern supported. Components: Accordion, Avatar, Carousel, Checkbox, Clipboard, Code Highlight, Collapsible, Color Picker, Combobox, Context Menu, Data List, Date Picker, Dialog, Drawer, Editable, File Upload, Float-Positioning, Hover Card, Menu, Number Input, Pagination, Pin Input, Popover, Progress, QR Code, Radio Group, Rating Group, Segment Group, Select, Signature Pad, Skeleton, Slider, Splitter, Steps, Switch, Table, Tags Input, Timer, Toast, Toggle, Toggle Group, Tooltip, Tour, Tree View.

title: Base UI – From the Creators of Radix, Floating UI, Material UI
insights: Base UI 1.0 launched in early 2026 with 35 accessible components. Zero styles — behavior only. Created by team that built Radix (Pedro Duarte), Floating UI (Atanas Stoyanov), and Material UI (MUI team). Uses slot-based composition API. Improved APIs over Radix based on lessons learned. Long-term maintenance commitment from MUI.

title: React – Floating UI
insights: @floating-ui/react package. useFloating hook provides: x, y position, strategy (absolute/fixed), refs for reference and floating, middlewares (flip, shift, offset, size, autoPlacement, hide). useFloatingRootContext enables interaction hooks higher in tree than useFloating — unlocks external reference element patterns.

title: useInteractions – Floating UI
insights: Composable interaction hook that merges event handlers from multiple interaction hooks (useHover, useFocus, useClick, useDismiss, useRole). Returns getReferenceProps and getFloatingProps prop-getters. Architecture insight: interaction hooks are independent and composable rather than monolithic, allowing mix-and-match behavior like hover-then-keyboard-nav.

title: ariakit/ariakit – GitHub
insights: Diego Hernandez's library (ex-Reakit). Composite primitive is most sophisticated in the ecosystem — handles 2D grid navigation, virtualFocus, aria-activedescendant mode, and roving tabindex mode with a single unified API. Store-based state management (not React context) for performance. Supports render delegation like asChild.

title: chakra-ui/ark – GitHub (Ark UI)
insights: Ark UI from Chakra Systems. 45+ components. Built on @zag-js state machines. Supports React, Vue, Svelte, Solid with identical API. WCAG compliant, tested with real AT. Uses asChild prop like Radix. State machine approach (Zag) provides predictable behavior transitions instead of ad-hoc boolean flags. Strongest cross-framework primitive library in ecosystem.

title: chakra-ui/zag – GitHub
insights: Finite state machine engine powering Ark UI. Each component is a state machine with defined states, transitions, and side effects. Framework bindings: @zag-js/react, @zag-js/vue, @zag-js/solid, @zag-js/svelte. Machine outputs: prop getters for DOM elements and ARIA attributes. Uses Playwright for cross-framework behavioral parity tests. Framework-agnostic primitives foundation.

title: floating-ui/floating-ui – GitHub
insights: Positioning engine used internally by Radix, Base UI, and many others. ~3kB gzipped. Detects overflow/boundary collisions, auto-flips, shifts, and hides floating elements. React package adds: useFloating, useInteractions, useHover, useFocus, useClick, useDismiss, useRole (applies ARIA roles to trigger/floating), useListNavigation (keyboard list nav in floating), useTypeahead.

title: tailwindlabs/headlessui – GitHub
insights: 27k+ stars. React and Vue packages. Key files: focus-trap.ts (sentinel nodes approach for focus boundary), keyboard.ts (key code constants), use-outside-click.ts, use-transition.ts (machine-state-driven CSS transitions). Unlike Radix, uses React context for compound component communication without explicit Provider export.

title: Headless UI – Tailwind Labs
insights: Completely unstyled, fully accessible UI components by Tailwind Labs. React and Vue support. Components: Dialog, Disclosure, Listbox (Select), Menu (Dropdown), Popover, RadioGroup, Switch, Tabs, Transition, Combobox, Select. Full keyboard navigation, ARIA auto-management (aria-expanded, aria-selected, aria-checked, aria-labelledby, role). GitHub: 27k+ stars.

title: Combobox – Headless UI
insights: Headless UI Combobox implements ARIA 1.2 combobox pattern. Components: Combobox, ComboboxInput, ComboboxButton, ComboboxOptions, ComboboxOption. Arrow keys select options, Enter confirms, Escape closes. Supports nullable value and custom display value function for object-based options.

title: Listbox – Headless UI
insights: Listbox replaces native <select> with full styling freedom. ListboxButton, ListboxSelectedOption, ListboxOptions, ListboxOption. Roving tabindex on options. Typeahead support. Multiple selection via aria-multiselectable.

title: Architecture – React Spectrum
insights: Three-layer architecture: (1) react-stately — platform-agnostic state/logic, (2) react-aria — DOM/ARIA/event wiring for web, (3) react-spectrum — Adobe design system on top. Enables React Native and other platform targets from same state layer. Interaction model: unified pointer/keyboard/touch in usePress. Collection interface: virtual data source for keyboard nav without materializing all DOM nodes.

title: Date and Time Pickers for All – React Spectrum Blog
insights: Deep engineering post on how React Aria built i18n-correct date/time pickers supporting 13 calendar systems (Gregorian, Buddhist, Coptic, Ethiopian, Hebrew, Indian, Islamic variants, Japanese, Persian, Taiwanese, ROC). Explains the @internationalized/date package — framework-agnostic locale-aware date math library. Critical reading for teams handling international date inputs.

title: Internationalized – React Spectrum
insights: @internationalized packages: date (multi-calendar locale-aware date math), number (locale number formatting), message (ICU MessageFormat). These are framework-agnostic and used independently. @internationalized/date supports 13 calendar systems, ZonedDateTime, timezone-aware operations. Critical for any primitive handling dates — most libraries get this wrong. Extracted as standalone to benefit non-React users too.

title: Interactions Overview – React Aria
insights: Full interaction hook set: usePress (cross-device press with state), useLongPress (long press), useHover (desktop hover, ignores touch emulation), useMove (drag gestures + arrow key equivalent), useKeyboard, useFocus, useFocusWithin (React's equivalent of CSS :focus-within), useScrollWheel. All hooks normalize browser/AT/device inconsistencies.

title: useCalendar – React Aria
insights: Calendar primitive using role=grid for the month display. Each day is role=gridcell with button inside. Arrow keys navigate days. Page Up/Down change month. Shift+Page Up/Down change year. Alt+Page Up/Down change decade. Home/End go to first/last day of month. Handles RTL layouts for Hebrew/Arabic. Works with CalendarDate, CalendarDateTime, ZonedDateTime types from @internationalized/date.

title: useFocusVisible – React Aria
insights: Tracks whether the current focus interaction is keyboard-driven (show ring) or pointer-driven (hide ring). Maps to WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.13 Focus Appearance. Fires VisibilityChangeEvent globally so all components share one mode state, avoiding the need for per-component tracking.

title: useListBox – React Aria
insights: useListBox wires ARIA listbox semantics to custom DOM. Uses Collection interface for keyboard navigation (virtual list support). Paired with useOption (single item) and useListBoxSection (group). Key: listbox options cannot contain interactive content — use useGridList for that case. Supports aria-multiselectable, aria-disabled, selection mode (single/multiple/none).

title: usePress – React Aria
insights: Unified press handler across mouse, touch, keyboard (Enter/Space), and screen reader click. Prevents duplicate events from browser's mouse-after-touch emulation. Tracks isPressed state for visual feedback. Safari 17 fix: moved away from manual preventDefault-then-focus approach to allow browser-native focus on buttons.

title: useSelect – React Aria
insights: useSelect combines trigger button + hidden listbox popup. Arrow keys type-ahead even without opening popup. Arrow Down opens and moves to first item. Int.Collator for locale-aware typeahead. Must be combined with useListBox for the popup. Handles collapsed-state keyboard interactions separately from expanded-state.

title: React Stately – Adobe
insights: State-management companion to React Aria. Implements collection management, selection state, pagination, tree data, sorted data. Fully platform-agnostic — can run on web, react-native, or any renderer. Exposes useListState, useSelectState, useComboBoxState, useCheckboxGroupState, useCalendarState, useRangeCalendarState, etc.

title: Downshift – WAI-ARIA Combobox Primitives
insights: Kent C. Dodds' library, now community-maintained. Hooks: useCombobox (ARIA 1.2 pattern since v7), useSelect (native select replacement), useMultipleSelection (multi-select for both). useCombobox accepts getItemProps, getInputProps, getToggleButtonProps, getMenuProps, getComboboxProps. Prop-getter pattern predates hooks era but remains valid. Strict ARIA 1.2 compliance including aria-haspopup on input (not wrapper).

title: useCombobox – Downshift
insights: useCombobox manages: inputValue, isOpen, selectedItem, highlightedIndex. Provides getters for input, menu, items, toggle button, combobox wrapper. Supports controlled/uncontrolled for each state variable independently. Typeahead via onStateChange callback. Internationalized string matching using Intl.Collator.

title: Slot – Radix Primitives
insights: @radix-ui/react-slot exposes the Slot and Slottable utilities independently. Slot accepts one child, clones it, and merges props from parent. Slottable marks which child acts as the slot when composing multi-child structures. Used as the engine behind asChild in every Radix primitive.

title: Accordion Pattern – W3C APG
insights: Accordion headers use role=heading with aria-level. Button inside heading has aria-expanded and aria-controls pointing to panel. Enter/Space toggles panel. Tab moves between headers (no roving tabindex — each header is independently tabbable). Down/Up arrow optional shortcut between headers.

title: Date Picker Dialog Example – W3C APG
insights: Reference implementation: date input triggers a modal dialog containing a calendar grid. Grid uses role=grid with role=gridcell buttons. Previous/next month navigation. aria-label on each day button includes full date. Arrow keys navigate days. Enter/Space selects. Escape closes. Focus returns to date input trigger.

title: Disclosure (Show/Hide) Pattern – W3C APG
insights: Simplest show/hide: button with aria-expanded and aria-controls. No role=heading required (unlike accordion). Enter/Space toggle. Used for navigation menus, FAQs, and inline expandable sections. Examples include disclosure navigation and hybrid top-level links+disclosure.

title: Grid Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=grid, role=row, role=gridcell. Two-dimensional navigation: Arrow keys in 4 directions. Home/End within row, Ctrl+Home/End for first/last cell overall. Page Up/Down for block scroll. Supports interactive cells. GridCells can contain interactive elements that receive focus directly. Alternative: aria-activedescendant on grid container.

title: Listbox Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=listbox, role=option. aria-multiselectable for multi-select. aria-selected on options. Supports both roving tabindex (recommended) and aria-activedescendant. Arrow keys navigate. Space selects. Shift+Arrow extends selection. Home/End first/last. Type-ahead search. When listbox is a popup, focus must remain in the combo input with aria-activedescendant.

title: Menu Button Pattern – W3C APG
insights: CRITICAL distinction: role=menu is for application menus (File/Edit/View bars), NOT for navigation links. Menu uses roving tabindex or aria-activedescendant. Arrow Down opens and moves to first item. Arrow Up opens and moves to last item. Escape closes and returns focus to trigger. Letter keys type-ahead. This distinction is widely misused in the wild.

title: Radio Group Example Using Roving tabindex – W3C APG
insights: Canonical implementation of roving tabindex. role=radiogroup container. Each radio: role=radio, tabindex=0 (selected) or tabindex=-1 (not selected). Arrow keys cycle selection AND move focus simultaneously. Space selects focused item. Tab exits radiogroup. Key: only ONE item has tabindex=0 at any time — the currently selected item.

title: Tabs Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=tablist, role=tab, role=tabpanel. aria-selected on active tab. aria-controls links tab to panel. aria-labelledby links panel to tab. Arrow Left/Right navigate tabs (roving tabindex). Optional: automatic activation on arrow key vs. manual activation on Enter/Space. Tab key moves into panel. Home/End supported.

title: Tree View Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=tree, role=treeitem, role=group for subtrees. aria-expanded on parent items. aria-selected for selection. aria-level, aria-setsize, aria-posinset for position context. Arrow Right expands or moves to first child. Arrow Left collapses or moves to parent. Home/End jump to first/last item. Typeahead. aria-activedescendant alternative to roving tabindex for tree containers.

title: Zag – State Machine UI Primitives
insights: Framework-agnostic state machine primitives by Segun Adebayo (Chakra UI author). Each UI pattern implemented as an XState-inspired finite state machine. Machines: accordion, clipboard, color-picker, combobox, date-picker, dialog, editable, file-upload, floating-panel, hover-card, menu, number-input, pagination, pin-input, popover, presence, progress, radio-group, rating-group, segment-group, select, signature-pad, slider, splitter, steps, switch, tabs, tags-input, timer, toast, toggle-group, tooltip, tour, tree-view, zagjs.

title: Releases – Base UI
insights: Release notes tracking Base UI component additions. 35 components at 1.0. Built by intersection of Radix/Floating UI/MUI talent — rare concentration of primitives expertise. Tailwind, CSS Modules, and CSS-in-JS all supported without assumptions.

title: downshift-js/downshift – GitHub
insights: 12k+ stars. Shows evolution of headless pattern: original render-prop Downshift → hooks useCombobox/useSelect/useMultipleSelection. Test suite is particularly valuable — shows what ARIA compliance actually requires in terms of DOM state transitions. Prop-getter pattern explicitly merges caller and library event handlers.

title: focus-trap/focus-trap – GitHub
insights: Vanilla JS focus trap used by many React wrappers. Key features: checkCanFocusTrap promise for animated entry delays, pause/unpause for nested traps, clickOutsideDeactivates, escapeDeactivates, returnFocusOnDeactivate. Uses tabbable package for focusable element detection. Important: UMD build does NOT bundle tabbable as a dependency.

title: focus-trap/tabbable – GitHub
insights: Low-level utility returning ordered array of tabbable DOM nodes. Uses Element.getClientRects() for visibility detection. Handles tabindex=0, positive tabindex, focusable-but-not-tabbable distinction. Shadow DOM support via option flag. displayCheck option: 'full' (default, uses getClientRects), 'non-zero-area', 'legacy-full', or 'none'. Dependency of focus-trap, react-focus-lock, and many headless libraries.

title: kobaltedev/kobalte – GitHub
insights: SolidJS headless UI toolkit. Built on SolidJS primitives (@solid-primitives). Brings Radix-style compound component DX to SolidJS ecosystem. WAI-ARIA APG compliant. Each component exports Root, Trigger, Content, etc. sub-components. Strong alternative to Radix for SolidJS design systems.

title: melt-ui/melt-ui – GitHub (Svelte)
insights: Builder API for Svelte headless components. Builder function returns object of element props that can be spread onto any HTML element: {builder: createDialog()}, then bind with use:melt. Lowest-level API in Svelte ecosystem. WAI-ARIA compliant. Components: Accordion, AlertDialog, Avatar, Checkbox, Collapsible, Combobox, ContextMenu, DatePicker, Dialog, DropdownMenu, Label, Menubar, Pagination, Popover, Progress, RadioGroup, ScrollArea, Select, Separator, Slider, Switch, Table, Tags Input, Tabs, Toast, Toggle, ToggleGroup, Toolbar, Tooltip, Tree.

title: theKashey/react-focus-lock – GitHub
insights: React focus trap used by Atlassian AtlasKit, Reach UI, Storybook. Watches focus behavior (intercepting focus events) rather than keyboard emulation. Supports nested locks (pause outer when inner activates). returnFocus option restores focus on unmount. Used when animated modals need a different lifecycle than synchronous focus-trap. sideCar pattern externalizes heavy DOM operations.

title: unovue/reka-ui – GitHub
insights: Source for Reka UI (ex Radix Vue). Demonstrates porting Radix primitives to Vue Composition API. Key challenge: mapping React's useRef/useEffect to Vue's reactive references and watchEffect. asChild implemented via Slot component. Controlled/uncontrolled via v-model. Teleport (Vue's portal) for overlays.

title: Dropdown Menu – Headless UI
insights: Headless UI Menu: Menu, MenuButton, MenuItems, MenuItem. role=menu on panel, role=menuitem on items. Roving tabindex within open menu. Arrow Down opens and focuses first item. Escape closes and returns to trigger. Character typeahead. Supports MenuItem as={link}. Note: menu role = application menu, NOT navigation list — critical ARIA distinction.

title: Tabs – Headless UI
insights: TabGroup, TabList, Tab, TabPanels, TabPanel. Horizontal/vertical orientation. Arrow-key navigation with optional automatic activation. aria-selected, aria-controls, role=tablist/tab/tabpanel wired automatically.

title: Kobalte – SolidJS Headless UI
insights: WAI-ARIA APG-compliant headless UI toolkit for SolidJS. Composable, granular component parts similar to Radix. Components: Accordion, Alert, AlertDialog, Breadcrumbs, Button, Checkbox, Combobox, ContextMenu, DatePicker, Dialog, DropdownMenu, HoverCard, Image, MenuBar, NumberField, Pagination, Popover, Progress, RadioGroup, Select, Separator, Skeleton, Slider, Switch, Table, Tabs, TextField, Toast, Toggle, ToggleGroup, Tooltip. Key value: SolidJS reactivity model gives fine-grained updates without virtual DOM overhead.

title: Melt UI – Svelte Headless Component Builders
insights: Builder API is distinct from compound components: createDialog() returns {trigger, overlay, content, title, description, close, portal} objects, each being a set of attributes to spread. No JSX component tree required. Integrates with Svelte's reactive paradigm. Builder functions are plain JavaScript — testable in isolation without rendering. WAI-ARIA APG compliant.

title: useGridList – React Aria
insights: role=grid for lists that contain interactive items (buttons, checkboxes, links within each row). Each row is role=row, each item is role=gridcell. useGridListItem enables focus management within rows. Different from useListBox: listbox items cannot have interactive content — gridlist rows can contain rich interactive content. Arrow keys navigate rows. Tab navigates within a row.

title: useToast – React Aria
insights: React Aria's toast primitive. Uses aria-live region for announcements. Manages toast queue state with useToastState. Exposes useToast (single item) and useToastRegion (list container). Programmatic API: add, close. Priority queue: 'high' interrupts immediately (aria-live=assertive), 'low' waits (aria-live=polite). Key: toasts must not auto-dismiss if they contain interactive content.

title: Reka UI – Vue Port of Radix Primitives
insights: Reka UI (formerly Radix Vue) is the Vue 3 port of Radix Primitives. 311+ projects on npm. Maintains API parity with Radix. Supports asChild via Slot component. WAI-ARIA compliant: keyboard navigation, focus management, roving focus, aria-* automation. Latest version 2.9.5 as of April 2026. Used by Nuxt UI (replaced Headless UI). 55+ primitives.

title: One-Time Password Field – Radix Primitives
insights: New 2025 primitive in preview. OTP field implemented as input elements inside a role=group container. Arrow-key navigation between inputs. Paste auto-distributes across all slots regardless of focused input. Focus automatically advances to next input on character entry. Addresses a missing pattern in most design systems.

title: Password Toggle Field – Radix Primitives
insights: 2025 Radix primitive for password inputs with show/hide toggle. Returns focus to input on visibility toggle. Resets to hidden state after form submission. Demonstrates thoughtful accessibility detail: focus restoration is often overlooked in custom implementations.

title: Carousel Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=region with aria-label on carousel container. Previous/next buttons outside the rotation mechanism. aria-roledescription='slide' on each slide. aria-label='N of M' on each slide for context. Auto-rotation must pause on keyboard focus or hover. Tab key navigates to any focusable content within visible slide. Rotation control (play/pause) button.

title: Data Grid Examples – W3C APG
insights: Three datagrid examples: basic, sortable, scrollable. role=grid, role=rowgroup, role=row, role=columnheader, role=rowheader, role=gridcell. Sortable columns: aria-sort='ascending'/'descending'/'none' on columnheader. Edit mode: Enter activates cell editor, Escape cancels, F2 alternative. Demonstrates aria-rowcount, aria-rowindex, aria-colcount, aria-colindex for virtualized/scrollable grids.

title: Slider Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=slider. aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuetext (for non-numeric representations). Arrow keys increment/decrement by step. Page Up/Down for larger steps. Home/End for min/max. Multi-thumb sliders use separate elements each with role=slider.

title: SpinButton Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=spinbutton for constrained numeric/date value input. aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax. Arrow Up/Down increment/decrement. Page Up/Down large step. Home/End min/max. The date picker spin button example was deprecated — prefer the dialog-based calendar or combobox-based date picker.

title: Switch Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=switch. aria-checked: 'true' (on) or 'false' (off). Different from checkbox: switch represents on/off binary state with immediate effect (e.g., enable dark mode), while checkbox is form input for list selection. Space key toggles. Focus handled as standard button. Accessible name required via aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible label.

title: Toolbar Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=toolbar. Roving tabindex — only one item in tab sequence at a time. Arrow Left/Right (horizontal) or Up/Down (vertical, aria-orientation=vertical). Home/End. When toolbar has a focused control, Tab moves focus out to next tabbable element after toolbar. Toolbar is single tab stop for entire group.

title: Tooltip Pattern – W3C APG
insights: Tooltip: popup with role=tooltip. Reference element uses aria-describedby pointing to tooltip id. NOT role=dialog. Shows on hover and keyboard focus. Hides on Escape, mouse out, blur. Key APG distinction: tooltip only describes — it cannot contain interactive elements. For actionable popups use Dialog or Popover (no standard ARIA role yet for popover, use role=dialog or region).

title: Landmark Regions – W3C APG
insights: Landmark roles: banner, complementary, contentinfo, form, main, navigation, region, search. Multiple nav/form regions must have unique aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigate by landmarks with dedicated keyboard shortcuts. Reduces cognitive overhead for AT users skimming page structure.

title: Introduction – Bits UI (Svelte)
insights: Bits UI provides Radix-style compound component DX on top of Melt UI builders for Svelte. Ergonomic API familiar to Radix users. Accessibility built-in from Melt UI. Keyboard navigation, screen reader support, focus management. Theme builder available. Figma design kit. Used by shadcn-svelte.

title: @kobalte/core – npm
insights: SolidJS Kobalte core package. 30+ components. Granular sub-component exports: Accordion.Root, Accordion.Item, Accordion.Trigger, Accordion.Content. Polymorphic as prop (SolidJS doesn't need asChild — as prop is type-safe via SolidJS's component model). Strong alternative for SolidJS design systems needing WAI-ARIA compliance.

title: Breadcrumb Pattern – W3C APG
insights: Breadcrumb wrapped in <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'>. Ordered list structure. Current page link has aria-current='page'. Structured as <ol> for correct enumeration in AT.

title: usePress.ts – React Aria Source
insights: Source implementation of React Aria's unified press handler. Shows: (1) pointerdown for mouse/touch start, (2) prevents click if pointer moved off target, (3) keyboard Enter/Space as press equivalents, (4) prevents double-firing from pointer+mouse event combos on touch devices, (5) isPressed state for visual feedback, (6) long press timeout via global pointer capture. Essential reading for understanding cross-input press semantics.

title: Headless Component: a pattern for composing React UIs – Martin Fowler
insights: Canonical description of the headless component pattern. Key insight: separates behavior+state from rendering. Shows evolution from monolithic component → render prop → hooks. Cites Downshift, React Table, React UseGesture, React ARIA, Headless UI as exemplars. Rule: 'if you can expose a hook, prefer it over a headless component' — hooks are more composable.

title: Building a Button Part 3: Keyboard Focus Behavior – React Aria Blog
insights: Deep dive into cross-browser button focus behavior. Safari quirk: buttons/inputs only focus on click if they have tabIndex. React Aria's historical approach: call preventDefault then manually focus. Newer approach (post Safari 17): remove preventDefault to let browser manage focus natively. Explains why usePress is non-trivial to implement correctly across browser/AT matrix.

title: aria-activedescendant is not focus – Sarah Higley
insights: Deep technical analysis: aria-activedescendant tells the accessibility tree which element is 'active' but does NOT move DOM focus. Consequences: (1) Auto-scroll doesn't happen (developer must implement), (2) VoiceOver doesn't read the active descendant on combobox rows — major AT compatibility gap. Recommendation: prefer roving tabindex for tree views and grids; use aria-activedescendant only for combobox input scenarios where input focus must stay.

title: WAI-ARIA 1.3 Working Draft – W3C
insights: First Public Working Draft published January 23, 2024. New roles: suggestion, comment, mark, code, time, image (alias for img). New attributes: aria-description (prose description, not label), aria-braillelabel, aria-brailleroledescription. aria-details now accepts multiple IDREFs. Not yet a standard — watch for implementation in AT.

title: What's New in WCAG 2.2 – W3C WAI
insights: WCAG 2.2 published October 5, 2023. 9 new success criteria. Key for primitives: SC 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured (AA) — focused element must not be entirely hidden by sticky headers/banners. SC 2.4.12 Focus Not Obscured Enhanced (AAA). SC 2.4.13 Focus Appearance (AAA) — focus indicator area ≥ 2px perimeter, contrast ratio ≥ 3:1. SC 2.5.7 Dragging Movements (AA) — all drag operations need pointer alternative. SC 3.2.6 Consistent Help (A).

title: Headless UI Alternatives: Radix vs React Aria vs Ark vs Base UI – LogRocket
insights: Structured comparison: Radix (28 components, React-only, asChild, mature), React Aria (40+ hooks, strict ARIA, i18n, Adobe backing), Ark UI (45+ cross-framework, state machines, Vue+Solid+Svelte), Base UI (35 components, zero styles, slot API, MUI backing). Key differentiator: Radix best for React-only, Ark UI best for multi-framework, React Aria best for maximum ARIA strictness.

title: aria-modal – MDN Web Docs
insights: aria-modal=true signals to AT that the element is a modal container — other content should be treated as inert. Condition for correct use: (1) application code prevents interaction with background, AND (2) visual styling obscures background. Important: aria-modal alone does NOT trap focus — focus trapping is a separate implementation concern. AT support is incomplete; inert attribute is preferred.

title: eBay MIND Patterns – Accessibility Patterns for the Web
insights: eBay's production-tested accessibility pattern library. Framework-agnostic (vanilla JS, MakeupJS). Progressive enhancement strategy. WCAG 2.2 Level AA. Patterns: Flyout, Tooltip, Dialog, Tabs, Fake Tabs, Menu, Listbox, Combobox, Carousel, Pagination, Skip Navigation, Infotip, Star Rating. Valuable for cross-framework implementation reference separate from React ecosystem.

title: Migrating from Radix to React Aria – Argos CI Blog
insights: Real-world migration report from Radix to React Aria. Key findings: React Aria has stricter ARIA compliance, catches more AT edge cases. Radix has better DX for quick assembly. React Aria requires more wiring code but produces more robust accessibility. React Aria's internationalization support was the deciding factor for Argos.

title: dequelabs/axe-core – GitHub
insights: 15M weekly npm downloads. Automated ARIA compliance testing engine. Covers WCAG 2.0/2.1/2.2 A, AA, AAA. Finds ~57% of WCAG issues automatically. Rules relevant to primitive testing: aria-required-attr, aria-valid-attr-value, aria-allowed-role, button-name, color-contrast, focus-trap (needs manual review), keyboard (needs manual review). Used by Storybook, Cypress, Playwright, Jest DOM.

title: Angular CDK a11y – Angular Material
insights: Angular CDK a11y package: FocusTrap directive (trap focus in element), cdkTrapFocusAutoCapture (auto-focus first item), LiveAnnouncer (aria-live region announcements), AriaDescriber (aria-describedby management), FocusMonitor (detect keyboard vs mouse focus), ListKeyManager (base for roving tabindex), FocusKeyManager (DOM focus), ActiveDescendantKeyManager (aria-activedescendant). Most complete framework-provided a11y primitive set.

title: The inert Attribute: Stop Writing Focus Trap JavaScript
insights: The HTML inert attribute (now baseline in all modern browsers as of 2024) makes all descendants non-interactive and invisible to AT without JavaScript. Replaces JavaScript focus traps for simpler cases. Caveats: no visual feedback (must style [inert] yourself), does not restore focus automatically, no nested-trap pattern. Native <dialog>.showModal() is still preferred — it handles both inert background + focus trap natively.

title: Spectrum Web Components – Adobe
insights: Adobe's Web Components implementation of Spectrum design system. v1.0.0 released October 31, 2024. Uses lit-element, Shadow DOM, CSS custom properties. RovingTabIndex utility for toolbar/listbox navigation. LiveAnnouncer for dynamic content. WAI-ARIA APG compliance. Framework-agnostic (pure web components). Roving tab index tool: @spectrum-web-components/tools/roving-tab-index.

title: Tooltips in the Time of WCAG 2.1 – Sarah Higley
insights: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.13: Content on hover or focus. Tooltip content must be (1) dismissable without moving pointer (Escape), (2) hoverable (pointer can move over tooltip without it disappearing), (3) persistent (stays visible). Most tooltip implementations fail (2). Article explains the 'safe triangle' cursor path technique for hover-on-tooltip. Critical for floating-ui/Radix tooltip implementations.

title: TanStack Table – Headless Table
insights: 100% headless — no DOM rendering. Cross-framework: React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, Qwik. Core features: sorting, filtering, pagination, grouping, column sizing, virtualization-ready, row selection. Accessibility: developers own the DOM so aria-rowcount, aria-rowindex, aria-colcount, aria-colindex must be manually added. Works with TanStack Virtual for large datasets.

title: TanStack Virtual – Headless Virtualizer
insights: Headless virtualization for lists, grids, tables. 60FPS rendering. Framework-agnostic. Accessibility requirement: set aria-rowcount on table to total row count (not visible row count) so screen readers report correct totals. useVirtualizer hook returns virtualItems array with index, start (pixel offset), size. Full control over DOM.

title: Anatomy of a Primitive – Vercel Academy
insights: Vercel's educational breakdown of Radix primitive anatomy. Covers: Root (context provider + state), Trigger (renders into button or asChild target), Content (floating element with portal), Item (repeating sub-elements), Indicator (visual state marker). Shows how compound component parts communicate through React context without prop drilling.

title: Radix vs shadcn/ui – WorkOS
insights: WorkOS (Radix maintainer) explains the relationship: Radix Primitives = headless behavior layer (npm dependency). shadcn/ui = pre-styled implementation of Radix + Tailwind that you copy into your codebase. shadcn/ui is NOT a library — it's a distribution mechanism. Accessibility comes from Radix; shadcn only adds styling. Key insight: Radix is the primitive, shadcn is the styled skin.

title: MUI Releases Base UI 1 with 35 Accessible Components – InfoQ
insights: Base UI 1.0 announcement. Highlights: built by team from Radix, Floating UI, Material UI backgrounds. 35 components. Slot-based composition API. Improved APIs over Radix based on real-world lessons. Zero styles. Long-term MUI maintenance. Positions against Radix directly as an evolution with better API ergonomics.

title: Implement Radix's asChild Pattern in React – Jacob Paris
insights: Explains how to implement asChild from scratch. Uses React.cloneElement to inject refs and props into child. Handles ref merging (composeRefs), event handler merging (prevent one overwriting another), and className merging. Shows why asChild is safer than 'as' prop: no complex generics needed, TypeScript type safety maintained for child element type.

title: Understanding SC 2.4.13: Focus Appearance – W3C WAI
insights: WCAG 2.2 AAA. Focus indicator area must be ≥ the perimeter of a 2 CSS pixel thick outline of the unfocused component. Contrast ratio ≥ 3:1 between focused and unfocused states. Exception: if the browser default focus indicator is used. Directly impacts all custom focus ring implementations in primitive libraries.

title: First Public Working Draft: WAI-ARIA 1.3 – W3C News
insights: W3C announcement of WAI-ARIA 1.3 FPWD (January 23, 2024). New roles and attributes added based on real-world AT usage patterns. Open for public comment period. Expected finalization timeline not yet set. Primitive library authors should monitor this for upcoming breaking changes in AT behavior expectations.

title: New Features in ARIA 1.3 – Orange Digital Accessibility
insights: Developer-focused breakdown of ARIA 1.3 changes. aria-description: distinct from aria-label (short name) — allows a longer prose description accessible only to AT. Example: tooltip text can be aria-description, while visible label is aria-label. role=mark for highlighted search results. role=suggestion for inline editing suggestions (Google Docs style). role=comment for annotation threads.

title: Roving Focus – Accessibility Solutions
insights: Visual explanation of roving focus pattern. Distinguishes roving tabindex from aria-activedescendant approach. Covers wrap-around behavior (last item → first on Arrow Right), disabled item skipping, and focus restoration after subtree collapse. Good mental model resource.

title: Building Accessible Components with Downshift – LogRocket
insights: Tutorial showing useCombobox and useSelect in practice. Demonstrates how prop-getter pattern requires explicitly spreading ALL returned getters onto correct DOM elements — missing one breaks ARIA. Shows state reducer pattern for custom behavior override. Typeahead implementation using highlightedIndex and items array.

title: React Slot/asChild Composition Pattern
insights: Explains Slot vs asChild conceptually. Slot is the utility; asChild is the prop interface. Alternative implementations: render prop pattern, as prop (legacy). Shows ref merging and prop merging code. Demonstrates how Slottable works when a component's children include a mix of Slot and non-Slot content.

title: cmdk – Command Menu React Component
insights: Fast, unstyled command menu (⌘K palette). Headless React component. Role=combobox. Handles search filtering, keyboard navigation, focus management, ARIA roles. Used by Vercel, Linear, Raycast, Planetscale. shadcn/ui includes it as Command component. The cmdk source is a reference implementation for accessible filtered list patterns.

title: Accessible Date Pickers – React DayPicker
insights: DayPicker v9 major accessibility update. WCAG 2.1 AA compliant. Automatic focus management when navigating calendar. Keyboard: Arrow keys navigate days, Shift+Arrows navigate months/years, Enter selects, Escape closes. Each day button has full date as aria-label. Internationalization via date-fns locale.

title: chakra-ui/ark DeepWiki
insights: Auto-generated architecture analysis of Ark UI. Shows how Zag state machines map to component APIs across frameworks. Each component: machine context = shared state, machine state = current behavioral state, machine transitions = what triggers state changes. Framework binding converts machine API to React hooks / Vue composables / Svelte stores.

title: Component Composition Pattern – radix-ui/primitives DeepWiki
insights: Auto-generated wiki from Radix source code. Explains how compound components use React context internally: Root creates context, sub-components consume it. Shows the pattern for creating custom compound component primitives following Radix conventions. Context is named after the component to enable detection of incorrect nesting.

title: ARIA Examples and Patterns – w3c/aria-practices DeepWiki
insights: Auto-generated index of all APG patterns with code-level analysis. Maps patterns to their example implementations and test cases. Useful for identifying which patterns have AT testing coverage and which are documented with working code only.

title: emilkowalski/vaul – GitHub (Drawer)
insights: Emil Kowalski's drawer component built on Radix Dialog + custom spring physics. 7.4k stars. Snap points. Background scaling. ARIA-friendly: inherits Radix Dialog's role=dialog, aria-modal, focus trap. Replaces native dialog on mobile. Used by Vercel in production. Ported to Svelte (vaul-svelte) and Vue. Touch event scroll handling is the primary complexity.

title: gpbl/react-day-picker – GitHub
insights: 12k+ stars. DayPicker v9 rewrites accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA, focus auto-management, proper grid semantics. Each day button: aria-label with full locale-formatted date. Navigation buttons: accessible names. Month grid: role=grid. Week row: role=row. Day cell: role=gridcell. Compatible with all date-fns locales for i18n.

title: awesome-react-headless-components – GitHub Curated List
insights: Curated list of React headless components. Key entries: Ariakit, Ark UI, Base UI, Dayzed (date picker primitives), Downshift, Floating UI, Headless UI, React Aria, Reach UI, React Hook Form, React Ranger (slider), react-roving-tabindex, React Table, React Virtual, Reakit. Useful as a coverage map for what headless patterns the ecosystem has solved.

title: stevejay/react-roving-tabindex – GitHub
insights: Focused React Hooks implementation of roving tabindex with grid support. useRovingTabIndex hook per item, plus useRovingTabIndexContainer for the container. Grid mode: third argument is zero-based row index. Arrow keys: Left/Right within row, Up/Down between rows, Ctrl+Home/End for absolute first/last. Demonstrates the wiring complexity that makes Ariakit's Composite valuable.

title: React UI Libraries in 2025 – Makers Den
insights: 2025 state of the ecosystem. shadcn/ui (90k+ stars, 250k+ weekly npm): code-ownership model, Radix + Tailwind. Radix: 18.6k stars, used by Vercel/Supabase. Base UI 1.0: MUI's unstyled layer. React Aria: Adobe backing, best i18n. Key trend: shadcn/ui's copy-paste model normalizing 'you own the code' approach to component libraries.

title: Roving Tab Index – Spectrum Web Components
insights: Adobe's standalone roving tabindex utility for web components. RovingTabindexController manages which item has tabindex=0. Listens for keyboard events (Arrow keys, Home, End, Page Up/Down). Updates tabindex attributes reactively. Wrapping behavior configurable. Demonstrates how to implement roving tabindex outside of React.

title: Reach UI – Accessible Foundation for React
insights: Ryan Florence's accessible React component foundation. 5.9k stars. Tested with Safari+VoiceOver, Firefox+NVDA, Edge+JAWS. Components: Accordion, Alert, AlertDialog, Combobox, Dialog, DisclosureButton/Panel, Listbox, Menu, Slider, Tabs, Tooltip. Less active since 2023 — Remix team moved on. Still valuable reference for multi-AT testing approach.

title: useDroppableCollection – React Aria
insights: React Aria's drag-and-drop primitive. Handles pointer drag, touch drag, and keyboard-accessible drag with Insert key. Screen reader virtual drag via drag-and-drop announcements. ARIA patterns: draggable items have aria-grabbed (deprecated but still needed for AT), drop targets announced via aria-live. Keyboard alternative: arrow keys to navigate, Enter to confirm drop. Fulfills WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.7 Dragging Movements.

title: November 2024 Release – React Spectrum
insights: November 2024 additions: Accordion and Disclosure components GA in React Aria and React Spectrum. DisclosureGroup for accordion behavior. New color picker suite: ColorPicker, ColorArea, ColorField, ColorSlider, ColorSwatch, ColorSwatchPicker, ColorWheel — all with accessible color descriptions for screen readers.

title: Reakit – Toolkit for Building Accessible UIs (Archived)
insights: Predecessor to Ariakit. Pioneered low-level ARIA primitive approach in React ecosystem. Composite, Tabbable, Rover, Disclosure, Dialog, Menu, Popover, Tab, Tooltip components. Now unmaintained — migrate to Ariakit. Historical value: shows design decisions that informed modern headless libraries. State hook + component hook architecture is still the pattern used in Ariakit.

title: Keyboard Navigation Patterns for Complex Widgets – UXPin
insights: Visual guide to keyboard patterns for: toolbar (roving tabindex), tree view (hierarchical arrow nav), grid (2D arrow nav), composite widgets (ARIA activedescendant). Explains when to use Tab vs Arrow keys, single tab stop principle, and how composite widgets appear as one focusable unit to keyboard users.

title: Mastering Accessible Modals with ARIA and Keyboard Navigation – A11y Collective
insights: Practical guide: dialog role + aria-modal + aria-labelledby + aria-describedby structure. Focus management checklist: move focus on open, trap Tab/Shift+Tab, return focus on close, Escape closes. Background content: apply inert or aria-hidden=true to everything outside dialog. Visual backdrop does not replace semantic aria-modal. Tested with NVDA+Chrome, VoiceOver+Safari.

title: A Look at the New WAI-ARIA 1.3 Draft – Craig Abbott
insights: Practitioner review of WAI-ARIA 1.3 FPWD. New roles: mark (highlighted text), suggestion (inline suggestion in text), comment, code (inline code semantics — different from pre/code HTML), time (semantic time element). aria-description allows rich prose description distinct from aria-label (short label). Braille-specific attributes for braille display users.

title: Accessibility Plugin – Embla Carousel
insights: Embla v9 Accessibility plugin adds ARIA to the bare-bones carousel engine. Provides accessible label for carousel container, slide position descriptions (N of M), and prev/next button ARIA attributes. useEmblaCarousel + AriaPlugin(). Without the plugin Embla is a positioning engine only — no semantics.

title: Radix UI vs React Aria Components – Fellipe Utaka
insights: Developer comparison: Radix wins on DX for compound component assembly. React Aria wins on ARIA strictness, internationalization, mobile-screen-reader support. Radix has better TypeScript inference for compound components. React Aria requires more render code but leaves less room for accessibility mistakes. Choice depends on team priority: speed (Radix) vs. compliance depth (React Aria).

title: React Roving Tabindex – Joshua Woodward
insights: Step-by-step implementation guide for roving tabindex in React. Shows useRef + useCallback approach for managing the active index, imperative focus calls, and keyboard handler. Demonstrates the pattern without external library dependencies. Valuable for understanding what libraries like Ariakit abstract away.

title: focus-trap – npm
insights: ~2M weekly downloads. Pure JS, framework-agnostic. Used by many React wrappers including focus-trap-react. Key API: createFocusTrap(element, options). Options: initialFocus, returnFocusOnDeactivate, escapeDeactivates, clickOutsideDeactivates, checkCanFocusTrap (promise for animation), onPostActivate, onPostDeactivate. Sentinel-node approach inserts invisible focusable nodes at boundaries.

title: react-focus-lock – npm
insights: ~1.5M weekly downloads. Unique: watches focus events rather than intercepting keyboard. Props: disabled, returnFocus, noFocusGuards, persistentFocus, crossFrame (for iframes), group (named group for nested locks). AutoFocusInside helper focuses first element in lock. RestoreFocus helper wraps trigger element for restoration. Side car pattern reduces main bundle.

title: Sonner – Opinionated Toast Component for React
insights: Emil Kowalski's toast library. Accessible: built-in ARIA, keyboard navigation, screen reader support. headless mode via useToaster hook for complete custom rendering. Stacked toast display with expand-on-hover. Dismissable via keyboard. Used by shadcn/ui. Key: useToaster hook is the headless primitive; Toaster component is the opinionated default.

title: Feed Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=feed for infinite scroll content streams (social media feeds). Articles have role=article with aria-posinset and aria-setsize. Arrow Up/Down navigates between articles. Focus moves to article not link. Page Up/Down move back/forward one page. Escape returns focus to page. Niche but important for social/news applications.

title: Navigation Landmark Example – W3C APG
insights: Implementation example: multiple navigation regions on same page each need unique aria-label. 'Main navigation', 'Site map', 'Breadcrumb navigation'. Without unique labels, screen readers cannot distinguish between them. aria-labelledby is preferred if a visible heading identifies the region.

title: Treegrid Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=treegrid combines tree structure with grid column navigation. Rows have role=row with aria-expanded for expandable rows. Cells have role=gridcell or role=rowheader/columnheader. Arrow keys: horizontal within row, vertical between rows, expand/collapse with Arrow Right/Left. Compound keyboard pattern for complex data hierarchies.

title: ally.js – JavaScript Accessibility Helper Library
insights: Low-level a11y utility: ally.maintain.disabled (renders subtree inert), ally.maintain.hidden (aria-hidden=true subtree), ally.query.focusable (all focusable elements), ally.query.tabbable (tab-ordered elements), ally.query.firstTabbable, ally.is.focusable, ally.is.tabbable. Predates the inert attribute. Maintained until ~2019 but still referenced. Use tabbable npm package for modern projects.

title: Authoring Practices Guide (APG) Examples and Rules in 2025 – Elementor
insights: Accessible overview of APG for non-specialists. Explains 60+ examples for 30+ pattern types. Useful onboarding resource for teams new to WAI-ARIA. Highlights that APG is guidance (not normative spec) and implementations must still test with real AT.

title: accessible-ui/popover – GitHub
insights: Accessible popover primitive for React. Manages aria-describedby associations, focus return on close, keyboard dismiss (Escape). Lightweight alternative to Floating UI for simple tooltip/popover cases. Part of accessible-ui collection which also includes @accessible/accordion, @accessible/drawer, @accessible/modal, @accessible/tabbable.

title: Navigating Accessibility: React Aria vs Radix UI – DhiWise
insights: Summary comparison: React Aria allows bypassing certain ARIA patterns with explicit options but encourages strict compliance. Radix provides more flexibility to ignore some accessibility rules. Key consideration: React Aria's strict mode catches edge cases that Radix defers to consumer. For healthcare/government/enterprise: React Aria. For startup design systems: Radix.

title: Window Splitter Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=separator with aria-orientation, aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuetext. Arrow keys resize panels. Home/End jump to minimum/maximum. Double-click or Enter toggles collapsed state. Focus on separator element itself. Rarely implemented but needed for resizable panel layouts (code editors, dashboards).
